title: The Coconut Palm
content_html:
The coconut palm

For millennia, the coconut has been central to the lives of Polynesian and Asian peoples. In the western world, on the other hand, coconuts have always been exotic and unusual, sometimes rare. The Italian merchant traveller Marco Polo apparently saw coconuts in South Asia in the late 13th century, and among the mid-14th-century travel writings of Sir John Mandeville there is mention of ‘great Notes of Ynde’ (great Nuts of India). Today, images of palm-fringed tropical beaches are clichés in the west to sell holidays, chocolate bars, fizzy drinks and even romance.

Typically, we envisage coconuts as brown cannonballs that, when opened, provide sweet white flesh. But we see only part of the fruit and none of the plant from which they come. The coconut palm has a smooth, slender, grey trunk, up to 30 metres tall. This is an important source of timber for building houses, and is increasingly being used as a replacement for endangered hardwoods in the furniture construction industry. The trunk is surmounted by a rosette of leaves, each of which may be up to six metres long. The leaves have hard veins in their centres which, in many parts of the world, are used as brushes after the green part of the leaf has been stripped away. Immature coconut flowers are tightly clustered together among the leaves at the top of the trunk. The flower stems may be tapped for their sap to produce a drink, and the sap can also be reduced by boiling to produce a type of sugar used for cooking.

Coconut palms produce as many as seventy fruits per year, weighing more than a kilogram each. The wall of the fruit has three layers: a waterproof outer layer, a fibrous middle layer and a hard, inner layer. The thick fibrous middle layer produces coconut fibre, ‘coir’, which has numerous uses and is particularly important in manufacturing ropes. The woody innermost layer, the shell, with its three prominent ‘eyes’, surrounds the seed. An important product obtained from the shell is charcoal, which is widely used in various industries as well as in the home as a cooking fuel. When broken in half, the shells are also used as bowls in many parts of Asia.

Inside the shell are the nutrients (endosperm) needed by the developing seed. Initially, the endosperm is a sweetish liquid, coconut water, which is enjoyed as a drink, but also provides the hormones which encourage other plants to grow more rapidly and produce higher yields. As the fruit matures, the coconut water gradually solidifies to form the brilliant white, fat-rich, edible flesh or meat. Dried coconut flesh, ‘copra’, is made into coconut oil and coconut milk, which are widely used in cooking in different parts of the world, as well as in cosmetics. A derivative of coconut fat, glycerine, acquired strategic importance in a quite different sphere, as Alfred Nobel introduced the world to his nitroglycerine-based invention: dynamite.

Their biology would appear to make coconuts the great maritime voyagers and coastal colonizers of the plant world. The large, energy-rich fruits are able to float in water and tolerate salt, but cannot remain viable indefinitely; studies suggest after about 110 days at sea they are no longer able to germinate. Literally cast onto desert island shores, with little more than sand to grow in and exposed to the full glare of the tropical sun, coconut seeds are able to germinate and root. The air pocket in the seed, created as the endosperm solidifies, protects the embryo. In addition, the fibrous fruit wall that helped it to float during the voyage stores moisture that can be taken up by the roots of the coconut seedling as it starts to grow.

There have been centuries of academic debate over the origins of the coconut. There were no coconut palms in West Africa, the Caribbean or the east coast of the Americans before the voyages of the European explorers Vasco da Gama and Christopher Columbus in the late 15th and early 16th centuries. 16th century trade and human migration patterns reveal that Arab traders and European sailors are likely to have moved coconuts from South and Southeast Asia to Africa and then across the Atlantic to the east coast of America. But the origin of coconuts discovered along the west coast of America by 16th century sailors has been the subject of centuries of discussion. Two diametrically opposed origins have been proposed: that they came from Asia, or that they were native to America. Both suggestions have problems. In Asia, there is a large degree of coconut diversity and evidence of millennia of human use – but there are no relatives growing in the wild.In America, there are close coconut relatives, but no evidence that coconuts are indigenous. These problems have led to the intriguing suggestion that coconuts originated on coral islands in the Pacific and were dispersed from there.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–8

Complete the table below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
trunk
making of ...

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 2:
"used as a replacement for endangered hardwoods in the furniture construction industry"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Bảng: timber for houses and the making of ...
→ timber dùng để làm nhà và đồ nội thất.

=> Đáp án: furniture

tableRows: timber for houses and the making of [1]
at the top of the trunk
stems provide sap, used as a drink or a source of [2]

a drink
a source of [6] for other plants

oil and milk for cooking and [7]
glycerine (an ingredient in [8])
_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.questionHtml:
THE COCONUT PALM
PartDescriptionUses
trunkup to 30 metrestimber for houses and the making of 1_______
trunkup to 6 metres longto make brushes
flowersat the top of the trunkstems provide sap, used as a drink or a source of 2_______
fruitsOuter layers
middle layer (coir fibresused for 3_______
Inner layer (shell)a source of 4_______
(when halved) for 5_______

a source of 6_______ for other plants

questions.question_text: trunk: up to 30 metres. Uses: timber for houses and the making of 1. ________

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
sap
source of ...

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 2:
"the sap can also be reduced by boiling to produce a type of sugar"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Nhựa cây → đun sôi → tạo đường.

=> Đáp án: sugar
questions.question_text: flowers: at the top of the trunk. stems provide sap, used as a drink or a source of 2. ________

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
coir fibres
used for ...

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 3:
"coir... particularly important in manufacturing ropes"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Sợi xơ dừa dùng nhiều để sản xuất dây thừng.

=> Đáp án: ropes

Bước 1: Keyword
shell
source of ...

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 3:
"An important product obtained from the shell is charcoal"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Vỏ dừa → tạo than.

=> Đáp án: charcoal

Bước 1: Keyword
when halved

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 3:
"the shells are also used as bowls"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Vỏ dừa bổ đôi → dùng làm bát.

=> Đáp án: bowls

Bước 1: Keyword
other plants

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 4:
"provides the hormones which encourage other plants..."

Bước 3: Phân tích
Nước dừa cung cấp hormone cho cây khác.

=> Đáp án: hormones
questions.question_text: coconut water: a drink; a source of 6. ________ for other plants

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
oil and milk

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 4:
"used in cooking... as well as in cosmetics"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Dầu và sữa dừa dùng trong nấu ăn và mỹ phẩm.

=> Đáp án: cosmetics
questions.question_text: coconut flesh: oil and milk for cooking and 7. ________

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
glycerine

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 4:
"nitroglycerine-based invention: dynamite"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Glycerine là thành phần của thuốc nổ dynamite.

=> Đáp án: dynamite

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 9–13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 9–13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
shade
germinate

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn 5:
"exposed to the full glare of the tropical sun, coconut seeds are able to germinate"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Bài nói hạt dừa nảy mầm dưới ánh nắng mạnh, không phải cần bóng râm.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: Coconut seeds need shade in order to germinate.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
transported
Asia → America

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn cuối:
"moved coconuts from South and Southeast Asia to Africa and then across the Atlantic to the east coast of America"

Bước 3: Phân tích
Chiều vận chuyển là Asia → America, đề nói ngược lại.

=> Đáp án: FALSE
questions.question_text: Coconuts were probably transported to Asia from America in the 16th century.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
west coast
different type

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn cuối chỉ nói:
"coconuts discovered along the west coast..."

Bước 3: Phân tích
Không có thông tin chúng khác loại với dừa ở bờ đông.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Coconuts found on the west coast of America were a different type from those found on the east coast.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
“All the coconuts found in Asia are cultivated varieties”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn cuối:

“In Asia, there is a large degree of coconut diversity and evidence of millennia of human use – but there are no relatives growing in the wild.”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Ở châu Á không có họ hàng dừa mọc hoang dã (“no relatives growing in the wild”), nghĩa là dừa ở đây đều gắn với sự canh tác và sử dụng của con người qua hàng nghìn năm.

=> Có thể suy ra dừa ở châu Á là các giống được con người trồng/cultivated.
=> Đáp án: TRUE
questions.question_text: All the coconuts found in Asia are cultivated varieties.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
cultivated differently
America and Pacific

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đọc đoạn cuối.

Bước 3: Phân tích
Chỉ nói về nguồn gốc và sự phân tán, không nói cách trồng khác nhau.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Coconuts are cultivated in different ways in America and the Pacific.
